6
Months of Us

Half a year of you rage-baiting me into oblivion, stealing my hoodies, and somehow still being the best part of my day, every day. Happy anniversary, my favorite person.

For You

love you to eternity

Six months today. Feels weird to say it out loud honestly, because it's gone so fast but also feels like we've been through so much already.

Remember when we first started talking that whole phase of just being curious about each other, figuring out if this was even a thing? Then it turned into full-on obsession mode, checking phone every two minutes waiting for your text. Everything about you felt new and exciting.

Then slowly the "getting to know you" phase kicked in I saw your actual moods, your stubbornness, the way you get when you're annoyed. And somehow I liked you more, not less.

Obviously after that came the not-so-fun part. We hit our differences hard. Fights, silent treatments, things said that we didn't fully mean, misunderstandings that blew up bigger than they should have. Honestly there were days I didn't know if we'd figure it out.

But we kept coming back. Every time, we'd sit down, actually talk, apologize when we messed up, and slowly rebuild whatever broke. That part wasn't easy but it mattered.

And now it feels different calmer. Like we've stopped trying to prove something to each other and just exist together. You're not just someone I'm dating, you're genuinely one of my closest people now. I can be dumb, moody, honest, whatever — with you, and it's fine.

Six months in, I think what I've realized is love isn't the exciting part at the start, it's this — choosing to stay even after the fights, even after we saw each other's worst days. That's what makes it real.

Thank you for not giving up on us. Here's to more of this, mess and all.

Yours Mayo (idk why u gave me that name is that bcuz of your love for mayonaise or idk pls enlighten me )

Serves: just the two of us

Recipe for Us (a.k.a. Mexican Rice, Extra Spicy)

Ingredients

  • 1 cup Mexican rice, because you'd fight someone over the last bowl
  • 2 tbsp chaos, fully unmeasured
  • A pinch of drama, for flavor
  • 1 whole heart (mine, previously used, still works great)
  • Unlimited rage bait, to taste

Method

  1. Combine one slightly chaotic girl with one slightly whipped boyfriend.
  2. Let simmer for 6 months, stirring occasionally with inside jokes.
  3. Add rice. Do not ask why, she just wants rice.
  4. Serve warm, preferably with her winning every argument.
  5. Repeat monthly, indefinitely.

Happy 6 Months, Princess.

Here's to every rice bowl and dosa nd food that we've shared, every argument you've won on purpose, and every month that somehow gets better than the last. I love you.
